name: Reviews.io
what_it_does: Reviews.io is a customer review collection and management platform with API access for sending review invitations, retrieving reviews and ratings, managing UGC, and syndicating Google Seller Ratings.
use_cases: Agents sending post-purchase review invitations via email or SMS automatically after order completion
Pulling company and product review data to display social proof widgets on storefronts
Syndicating reviews to Google Shopping for Seller Ratings to improve paid search performance
Monitoring incoming reviews and routing negative feedback to CX teams via webhooks
Aggregating review metrics across multiple stores or brands for reporting dashboards
not_for: Enterprise-scale ratings syndication across retail networks — Bazaarvoice has stronger retailer relationships
B2B software reviews — G2 is the right platform for software buyer trust
Complex loyalty or SMS marketing — Yotpo or Klaviyo handle those use cases better
best_when: You need Google Seller Ratings integration alongside review collection for an ecommerce brand at competitive pricing versus Trustpilot or Yotpo.
avoid_when: You need deeply integrated loyalty programs or advanced SMS marketing alongside reviews — Reviews.io is reviews-focused.

auth.notes: Simple API key authentication passed as a query parameter or header. Store ID required alongside API key for all requests. Single key covers all operations — no scope granularity.

pricing.notes: Straightforward tiered pricing based on monthly order volume and feature access. Cheaper than Trustpilot or Yotpo at similar tiers. Google Seller Ratings available on mid-tier and above.

performance.rate_limits: Not explicitly published; estimated 60–120 req/min based on user reports

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is relatively thin — endpoint coverage is incomplete and examples are sparse. Error responses lack detailed codes; often return generic HTTP status without actionable messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Review invitations can be deduplicated using order ID. No explicit idempotency keys for other operations.
known_agent_gotchas: Store ID must be passed with every request alongside the API key
Rate limits are not documented — agents should implement conservative throttling
No official SDK — agents must implement raw HTTP calls
Documentation gaps mean some endpoints require trial-and-error to understand response shapes
Webhook reliability varies — implement idempotent handlers and handle duplicate events

security_score_components.security_notes: GDPR compliant. TLS enforced. API key auth is simple but lacks scope granularity — a single leaked key exposes all operations. No OAuth or fine-grained access control.
